phrases.cultural_note: Entry to the cathedral is free, as with most churches in France.

phrases.text_local: Peut-on prendre des photos à l'intérieur ?
phrases.pronunciation: puh-TON PRAHN-druh day FOH-toh ah lan-tay-RYUHR
phrases.cultural_note: Photos without flash are usually allowed in French cathedrals, but always check for signs.

phrases.text_local: Quelles sont les heures de visite ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel son layz UHR duh vee-ZEET
phrases.cultural_note: The cathedral is open daily from 8am to 6pm. Mass is held at 10am and 6pm on Sundays.

responses.english: From 8 AM to 6 PM every day.
responses.phonetic: wee, may ruh-gar-DAY layz oh-REHR ah-fee-SHAY oh-zhoor-DWEE
responses.french: De huit heures à dix-huit heures tous les jours.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if the cathedral is free to enter
phrases.text_local: L'entrée de la cathédrale est-elle gratuite ?
phrases.pronunciation: lahn-TRAY duh lah kah-tay-DRAL ay-TEL grah-TWEET
phrases.cultural_note: La Major is one of the largest cathedrals built in France in the 19th century, combining Byzantine and Romanesque styles.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where to see the best view of the cathedral from outside
phrases.text_local: D'où voit-on le mieux la cathédrale de l'extérieur ?
phrases.pronunciation: doo vwah-TON luh MYUH lah kah-tay-DRAL duh leks-tay-RYUR

responses.english: From the esplanade facing the sea, north side.
responses.phonetic: duh les-plah-NAHD fahs ah lah MAIR, koh-TAY NOR.
responses.french: De l'esplanade face à la mer, côté nord.
